title: ctrl-c only shows ^C, not quit in containers
body:
### Issue Description

fresh installed podman, i just pull the basic alpine image, and run it:
`podman run --rm --init -it alpine:3.21 /bin/sh`
when i press ctrl-c, it shows like this:
```
/ # ^C

/ # ^C

/ # ^C

/ # ^C
```
but never quit the container, only the `exit` works
i tried --init, --stop-signal SIGKILL, even stty -icrnl before i run, still only ^C shows, no quit

`$ podman version`
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.6.1
API Version:   5.6.1
Go Version:    go1.25.1
Built:         Fri Sep  5 03:23:56 2025
Build Origin:  brew
OS/Arch:       darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.6.1
API Version:  5.6.1
Go Version:   go1.24.6
Git Commit:   1e2b2315150b2ffa0971596fb5da8cd83f3ce0e1
Built:        Thu Sep  4 08:00:00 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run --rm --init -it alpine:3.21 /bin/sh
2. press ctrl-c
3.


### Describe the results you received

only ^C chars

### Describe the results you expected

quit the container

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

iTerm2 with zsh

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
ctrc-c genertes a SIGINT signal but the shell does not exit on that.
To exit a shell gracefully use ctrl-d (to close the stdin).
